name: United Trades
description_short: United Trades Federal Credit Union is a member-owned credit union serving skilled trade workers with checking, savings, loans, and credit products designed for union members.
description_long:
United Trades Federal Credit Union (UTFCU) is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union operating in the Northwest region, specifically designed to serve skilled trade workers and union members. The institution positions itself as "banking built for those who build America," reflecting its core mission to support workers in construction, trades, and union-affiliated occupations. UTFCU is federally chartered and NCUA-insured, providing member protection and stability.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including checking and savings accounts (regular savings, holiday savings, retirement savings), certificate accounts, youth accounts, personal loans, auto loans, RV loans, mortgages, home equity lines of credit, and credit cards. They also provide specialized services such as a Union Vacation Fund for members to manage paid time off, investment alternatives, and financial coaching. Their online and mobile banking platforms allow members to manage accounts, pay bills, and deposit checks remotely.

What distinguishes United Trades from traditional banks is their deep understanding of union work cycles and irregular income patterns. They offer "Solidarity Support" programs that provide flexible options when work slows or life changes unexpectedly, acknowledging the unique financial challenges faced by trade workers. Their home loan expertise specifically addresses how union employment impacts mortgage qualification and financing. As a credit union, they operate on a not-for-profit basis, returning profits to members through better rates and lower fees.

While UTFCU offers solid financial products and member-focused service, potential members should note that membership eligibility is restricted to those with connections to skilled trades and unions. The website does not provide detailed rate comparisons or APR ranges beyond sample rates (auto loans as low as 5.50%, certificates as high as 3.75%, credit cards as low as 10.50%). Online and mobile banking recently experienced connectivity issues, and membership requirements may exclude some consumers.
services: Mortgages and Home Equity Lines of Credit
pros: Specializes in serving skilled trade workers and union members with understanding of work-cycle fluctuations
Offers flexible "Solidarity Support" programs for members during job transitions or financial hardship
Provides Union Vacation Fund, a unique service for managing paid time off funds
Home loan experts understand union employment and its impact on mortgage qualification
Not-for-profit structure means earnings return to members through better rates and lower fees
Full-service credit union with checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and credit cards
NCUA-insured member accounts with up to $250,000 protection per account
cons: Membership eligibility is restricted to skilled trade workers and union-affiliated individuals, limiting access
Website lacks detailed APR/rate schedules and comprehensive fee disclosures
Recent online and mobile banking connectivity issues reported, creating service disruption
Limited geographic presence (Northwest region only) may restrict branch access for some members
best_for: Skilled trade workers and union members seeking specialized financial services
Individuals buying homes while self-employed or in union trades with variable income
Workers needing flexible financial support during seasonal or cyclical job transitions
diagnosis: United Trades Federal Credit Union is best for union members and skilled trade workers seeking comprehensive banking services designed with their irregular income and work-cycle needs in mind. The main caveat is that membership eligibility is restricted to those with trade union or skilled trade affiliations, and applicants should verify eligibility requirements before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
